document_meta.title: 3M Acrylic Foam Tape GT7100 Series Technical Data Sheet

sections.text: 3M Acrylic Foam Tape GT7100 Series offers very good adhesion performance and high flexibility. These tapes are specifically designed for the attachment of automotive exterior and interior trim applications.
sections.source_text: 3M™ Acrylic Foam Tape GT7100 Series offers very good adhesion performance and high flexibility. These tapes are specifically designed for the attachment of automotive exterior and interior trim applications.

3M Tape GT7100 Series offers the following key features: Provides final adhesion and holding power to automotive paints and substrates. Meets a number of OEM specifications. Follows the shrinkage and elongation of the plastic part caused by temperature change and has good stress relaxation properties. Stress relaxation characteristics of the viscoelastic core, allow the tape to conform well to complex geometries and deliver excellent wet out. Demonstrates excellent adhesive performance at various temperature conditions. Excels in a variety of weather conditions and offers solvent and high temperature resistance.
text_items: Provides final adhesion and holding power to automotive paints and substrates.
Meets a number of OEM specifications.
Follows the shrinkage and elongation of the plastic part caused by temperature change and has good stress relaxation properties.
Stress relaxation characteristics of the viscoelastic core allow the tape to conform well to complex geometries and deliver excellent wet out.
Demonstrates excellent adhesive performance at various temperature conditions.
Excels in a variety of weather conditions and offers solvent and high temperature resistance.

sections.text: Typical performance of 3M Acrylic Foam Tape Series GT7100 is shown below. Peel and shear values depend on substrate characteristics and/or paint composition. These values are for reference only, and not to be used for specification purposes. For specification values please see the certificate of analysis.

These values are for reference only, and not to be used for specification purposes. For specification values please see the certificate of analysis.

sections.text: Three years from date of manufacture when stored at recommended storage condition 23°C +/- 2°C and 50% +/- 4% relative humidity. 3M Acrylic Foam Tape GT7100 Series should be stored in typical storage conditions for pressure-sensitive adhesives used within the automotive industry. During transportation, storage conditions are uncontrolled for a short period of time (a few days or weeks). This should not impact product performance. However, materials should not be stored in excess of the recommended conditions for extended periods.

candidate_facts.source_text: 180° Peel Adhesion to Painted Panel ... Typical Performance Value (N/cm)
candidate_facts.mapping_reason: Peel adhesion values are measurable performance facts with substrate and state. Dictionary has only generic 'foil_peel_strength' in N/25mm, but document unit is N/cm and metric is 180° peel adhesion; keep as candidate fact pending dictionary normalization.

This is a condition-aware shear performance matrix, but it is tensile/shear strength in MPa, not explicitly 70°C static shear holding time in hours. Alias overlap exists with shear, but unit/type mismatch prevents high-confidence direct mapping to static_shear_70c.

methods.source_text: Conditions/Test Parameter Definitions: Immediate State = 23°C, 20 minutes ... Wax-remover immersion = 23°C, 24 hours -> Wax-remover, 1 hour -> 23°C, 24 hours
180° Peel Adhesion to Painted Panel Tape Size: 25 mm width; Tape backing: 25 μm PET film; Rolling Pressure: 5 kg roller one-way; Peel Speed: 50 mm/min.

notes: Painted panel: white colored painted panel with advanced paint, used on a vehicle.
PVC panel: 3M Adhesion Promoter N-210NT is applied on the PVC panel.

methods.source_text: Shear Strength Tape Size: 25 mm x 25 mm; Rolling Pressure: 5 kg roller one-way; Tensile Speed: 50 mm/min. Test measures liner side adhesion to painted panel and non-liner side adhesion to PVC panel.

details.adhesion_setup: Test measures liner side adhesion to painted panel and non-liner side adhesion to PVC panel.

unresolved.reason: Prompt contains conflicting output schema requirements: first requests top-level keys document_meta/sections/tables/candidate_items/candidate_facts/candidate_content/methods/unresolved, later requests exactly six top-level buckets series/items/params/category_params/spec_values/unmatched. Current output follows the first explicit schema.

Dictionary snapshot includes liner_option_pet and liner_option_sc2 only. Document states polyethylene liner, not PET or SC2 liner option. Should remain unmatched or as descriptive construction/storage content unless broader liner parameter exists.

Table header says 'Core Color'. This is likely product-searchable color, but could specifically mean foam core color rather than final visible product color. Mapped with moderate confidence to color main field; review recommended.
No separate adhesive thickness provided; only total thickness and construction layers are present.

Document uses 'Acrylic Foam Core'; dictionary key exists for base_material, but normalized vocabulary value may need review against existing master data naming such as acrylic foam / foam / foam core.
Matrix visually implies shared liner and adhesive across item codes, but row-grouping layout could omit exceptions. Inferred as series-wide/item-group-wide with good but not absolute confidence.
unresolved.source_text: Physical Properties ... Liner ... Adhesive ... grouped items
unresolved.candidate: Shared liner/adhesive/base construction across all items

unresolved.reason: Available dictionary key static_shear_70c expects static shear/shear holding, preferred unit hour. Document provides shear strength in MPa under multiple conditions, not direct static shear holding time at 70°C. Do not force-map.

Dictionary snapshot includes foil_peel_strength preferred unit N/25mm, but document gives 180° peel adhesion to painted/PVC panels in N/cm. Unit and test naming mismatch require review before final dictionary mapping.
No competitor equivalents, replacement hints, or substitute notes found in provided excerpt.
Only general automotive exterior/interior trim application is explicit; no distinct special applications section found.
Regulatory information exists but does not clearly map to current required buckets or whitelisted main fields in this task. Could be retained as unmatched note if needed.
